title: zapoteca aculeata
intro: Zapoteca aculeata, also known as the Zapote or Catclaw, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, providing relief and promoting overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Zapoteca aculeata include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be made by steeping the plant's parts in hot water to harness its medicinal benefits.
